title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 24, 2026: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Markets

items.headline: U.S. Equities Retreat Amid Renewed Middle East Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. stock markets pulled back on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, as earlier optimism regarding a potential de-escalation of Middle East tensions faded, replaced by concerns over the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and its impact on global oil supplies. This shift in sentiment led to increased market volatility, with defensive sectors showing relative strength while technology and growth stocks faced downward pressure. Rising crude oil prices amplified inflation fears, further contributing to the cautious mood among investors.

items.statContext: Tech-heavy index declined as growth stocks faced pressure.

items.headline: Paranovus Entertainment Technology Leads Gainers
items.summary: Paranovus Entertainment Technology Ltd. surged as one of the day's top performers.

items.headline: Robin Energy Sees Significant Gains
items.summary: Robin Energy Ltd. experienced a strong upward movement.

items.headline: AleAnna, Inc. Jumps Over 40%
items.summary: AleAnna, Inc. recorded substantial gains during Tuesday's trading.

items.headline: Outlook Therapeutics Tumbles on Public Offering
items.summary: Shares of Outlook Therapeutics plunged after the company priced a public offering of shares and warrants.

items.headline: Estée Lauder Slumps Amid Merger Talks
items.summary: Estée Lauder shares fell significantly following confirmation of discussions regarding a potential business combination.

items.headline: Microsoft Declines as Tech Faces Headwinds
items.summary: Microsoft shares declined as technology stocks continued to face pressure from rising interest rates and inflation concerns.

items.headline: Energy and Defensive Sectors Lead Amid Market Rotation, Tech Lags
items.summary: On Tuesday, Energy, Utilities, and Communication Services emerged as leading sectors, driven by rising commodity prices and a defensive rotation as investors sought havens amid economic uncertainty. Conversely, Real Estate and Consumer Discretionary sectors lagged. The technology sector also underperformed, with higher-for-longer interest rate expectations undermining valuation multiples for growth stocks. Risk-sensitive AI companies, in particular, saw sharp declines, while defensive stocks like Walmart (+2%) gained, supporting the Dow.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Released, Fed's Stance on Rates Shapes Outlook
items.summary: Today's economic calendar included the release of U.S. Productivity (4Q F) and the Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index for March. S&P Global's flash U.S. Services and Manufacturing PMI for March were also closely watched. The Federal Reserve, following its mid-March meeting, maintained interest rates in the 3.5%-3.75% range, signaling a 'wait-and-see' approach amidst persistent inflation and geopolitical uncertainty. The FOMC upgraded its 2026 GDP growth forecast to 2.4% while raising inflation expectations to 2.7%. Notably, market expectations have shifted, now pricing in zero rate cuts by the end of 2026, a significant change from earlier projections.

items.headline: Core & Main Beats EPS, Smithfield Foods Reports Strong Quarter
items.summary: Core & Main Inc. ($CNM) reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings of $0.52 per share, surpassing consensus estimates, though its revenue of $1.58 billion fell slightly short of expectations. Smithfield Foods ($SFD) also announced a strong fourth quarter, delivering a 'double beat' on earnings and revenue, leading to positive pre-market activity for its shares. Video game retailer GameStop ($GME) was among other companies expected to report earnings today.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher, Fueling Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict, remained a dominant macro trend influencing markets. Mixed signals regarding diplomatic efforts contributed to market jitters and heightened concerns about disruptions to global oil supplies. This uncertainty propelled crude oil prices higher, with Brent crude surging towards $110 per barrel and WTI Crude Oil jumping 3.87% to $91.54. The surge in oil prices further exacerbated inflation fears, impacting investor positioning across various sectors. Concurrently, U.S. Treasury yields continued their upward trend, with the 10-year yield reaching approximately 4.37% and the 2-year yield at 3.88%.
